attributes.description: Alerts when GPU utilization sustains above 95% over 15 minutes. Saturation may be desired for training, but for inference workloads it caps QPS.
attributes.name: [NVIDIA GPU OTel] GPU utilization saturated

investigation_guide.blob:
## NVIDIA GPU utilization saturated

### What fired
`AVG(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL) > 95%` over the 15-minute window.

### Why it matters
Sustained high utilisation is fine for batch training but for inference / serving it means tail latency is rising and capacity headroom is gone — any traffic burst will queue.

### Triage
1. Determine the workload type (training vs inference).
2. For inference: check request queue depth and latency.
3. Inspect framebuffer usage and clock throttling for compounding issues.

### Remediation
- Scale out by adding GPUs / nodes.
- Optimise model (quantisation, batching) to reduce GPU time per request.

### Tuning
- 95% threshold; raise for training fleets where 100% is the goal.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-nvidia_gpu.otel-default
| WHERE DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_util = AVG(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL),
    max_util = MAX(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL)
  BY Hostname, UUID, modelName
// Sustained high utilization above 95% indicates compute saturation
// Adjust threshold based on workload expectations
| WHERE avg_util > 95
| SORT avg_util DESC
